stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • In 2021, museum educators at Oberlin College developed an interactive, online format to present an exhibit on Reconstruction-era newspapers.
  • Their “Voices of Reconstruction” website featured an interactive digital map of the southern United States.
  • Visitors were asked to focus on a region of their choice and select from that region a newspaper headline.
  • One click took them to scanned articles, audio readings, photographs, and contextual essays.
  • The exhibit was popular with visitors because the format let them follow their own curiosity through the material.
stem: The student wants to explain an advantage of the “Voices of Reconstruction” format. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: To access scanned articles, audio readings, photographs, and contextual essays, visitors had to first click on a headline.
Using an interactive format, museum educators at Oberlin College created “Voices of Reconstruction,” an exhibit on Reconstruction-era newspapers.
“Voices of Reconstruction” featured a digital map of the southern United States with several regions, each containing newspaper headlines.
The “Voices of Reconstruction” format appealed to visitors because it let them follow their own curiosity through the material.

rationale:
Choice D is the best answer. It explains an advantage of the format: it appealed to visitors by letting them follow their own curiosity.

Choice A describes how visitors interacted with the site without naming an advantage. Choice B mentions an interactive format but doesn’t state what advantage it offers. Choice C describes the map without explaining a format advantage.
